title: Fish Finger Tacos with Sweetcorn Salsa & Avocado Smash
summary: Crispy fish fingers tucked into soft tacos with zingy sweetcorn salsa, creamy avo smash and a punchy green sauce for the grown-ups.

method.description: 1 Adult Serving = 2 Kids ServingsCook the fish fingers in the oven following pack instructions.

Meanwhile, drain the sweetcorn. Peel and finely chop the red onion. Zest half of one lime and squeeze over a quarter of the juice. Toss everything together and season with salt and pepper — or leave some of the sweetcorn plain for fussy eaters.
Scoop the avocado into a bowl and mash with a quarter of the lime juice. Fold through half of the Greek yoghurt and season to taste.

To make the green sauce for the adults, add the coriander (stems and all, reserving a few leaves for garnish), pickled jalapeños (juice and all) and the remaining yoghurt to a blender. Blitz until smooth.
Warm the tortillas according to pack instructions.

Serve up! Kids Plate: Serve the fish fingers with a warm tortilla, a scoop of the sweetcorn salsa and a dollop of plain yoghurt on the side.Parents Plate: Spread the avocado smash over each tortilla. Top with a fish finger, a spoonful of salsa and a drizzle of the green jalapeño sauce. Scatter with reserved coriander leaves. Serve with lime wedges on the side.

chefs.name: Anna Helm Baxter
chefs.summary: Anna Helm Baxter is a cookbook author, food stylist, and food producer at Mob. Having spent most of her life in the US, she loves potlucks, tailgates, and a good Thanksgiving spread. As a working mum of three, she gets the dinner struggle, which is why she’s all about recipes that are joyful, healthy, and totally doable. And if all else fails she always has cookies stashed in the freezer.
